title: magnistipula butayei subsp. sargosii
intro: Magnistipula butayei subsp. sargosii, also known as the Sargos's Magnistipula, is a plant species that belongs to the family Magnciaceae and is native to the Philippines. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat fever and inflammation, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Magnistipula butayei subsp. sargosii include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction for the treatment of various ailments, and it is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures.
